volumeTitle: Children of Heracles. Hippolytus. Andromache. Hecuba
label: Hippolytus (Section 430 - Section 598)

sections.label: Section 430
sections.original: χρόνος · παρ ’ οἷσι μήποτ ’ ὀφθείην ἐγώ .
sections.translation: ’ Mongst such may I be never seen !

sections.label: Section 431
sections.original: φεῦ φεῦ · τὸ σῶφρον ὡς ἁπανταχοῦ καλὸν
sections.translation: Now look ! how fair is chastity however viewed , whose fruit is good repute amongst men .

sections.label: Section 433
sections.original: δέσποιν ’, ἐμοί τοι συμφορὰ μὲν ἀρτίως
sections.translation: My queen , ’ tis true thy tale of woe , but lately told , did for the moment strike me with wild alarm ,

sections.label: Section 435
sections.original: νῦν δ ’ ἐννοοῦμαι φαῦλος οὖσα · κἀν βροτοῖς
sections.translation: but now I do reflect upon my foolishness ; second thoughts are often best even with men . Thy fate is no uncommon one nor past one ’ s calculations ; thou art stricken by the passion Cypris sends . Thou art in love ; what wonder ? so are many more .

sections.label: Section 440
sections.original: κἄπειτ ’ ἔρωτος οὕνεκα ψυχὴν ὀλεῖς ;
sections.translation: Wilt thou , because thou lov ’ st , destroy thyself ? ’ Tis little gain , I trow , for those who love or yet may love their fellows , if death must be their end ; for though the Love-Queen ’ s onset in her might is more than man can bear , yet doth she gently visit yielding hearts ,

sections.label: Section 445
sections.original: ὃν δ ’ ἂν περισσὸν καὶ φρονοῦνθ ’ εὕρῃ μέγα ,
sections.translation: and only when she finds a proud unnatural spirit , doth she take and mock it past belief . Her path is in the sky , and mid the ocean ’ s surge she rides ; from her all nature springs ; she sows the seeds of love , inspires the warm desire

sections.label: Section 450
sections.original: οὗ πάντες ἐσμὲν οἱ κατὰ χθόν ’ ἔγγονοι .
sections.translation: to which we sons of earth all owe our being . They who have aught to do with books of ancient scribes , or themselves engage in studious pursuits , know how Zeus of Semele was enamoured ,

sections.label: Section 455
sections.original: ἡ καλλιφεγγὴς Κέφαλον ἐς θεοὺς Ἕως
sections.translation: how the bright-eyed goddess of the Dawn once stole Cephalus to dwell in heaven for the love she bore him ; yet these in heaven abide nor shun the gods ’ approach , content , I trow , to yield to their misfortune .

sections.label: Section 459
sections.original: σὺ δ ’ οὐκ ἀνέξῃ ; χρῆν σ ’ ἐπὶ ῥητοῖς ἄρα
sections.translation: Wilt thou refuse to yield ? thy sire , it seems , should have begotten thee on special terms

sections.label: Section 460
sections.original: πατέρα φυτεύειν , ἢ ’ πὶ δεσπόταις θεοῖς
sections.translation: or with different gods for masters , if in these laws thou wilt not acquiesce . How many , prithee , men of sterling sense , when they see their wives unfaithful , make as though they saw it not ? How many fathers , when their sons have gone astray ,

sections.label: Section 465
sections.original: συνεκκομίζειν Κύπριν ; ἐν σοφοῖσι γὰρ
sections.translation: assist them in their amours ? ’ tis part of human wisdom to conceal the deed of shame . Nor should man aim at excessive refinement in his life ; for [*] they cannot with exactness finish

sections.label: Section 470
sections.original: πεσοῦσ ’ ὅσην σὺ πῶς ἂν ἐκνεῦσαι δοκεῖς ;
sections.translation: e ’ en the roof that covers in a house ; and how dost thou , after falling into so deep a pit , think to escape ? Nay , if thou hast more of good than bad , thou wilt fare exceeding well , thy human nature considered .

sections.label: Section 473
sections.original: ἀλλ ’, ὦ φίλη παῖ , λῆγε μὲν κακῶν φρενῶν ,
sections.translation: O cease , my darling child , from evil thoughts , let wanton pride be gone , for this is naught else ,

sections.label: Section 475
sections.original: τάδ ’ ἐστί , κρείσσω δαιμόνων εἶναι θέλειν ·
sections.translation: this wish to rival gods in perfectness . Face thy love ; ’ tis heaven ’ s will thou shouldst . Sick thou art , yet turn thy sickness to some happy issue . For there are charms and spells to soothe the soul ; surely some cure for thy disease will be found .

sections.label: Section 480
sections.original: ἦ τἄρ ’ ἂν ὀψέ γ ’ ἄνδρες ἐξεύροιεν ἄν ,
sections.translation: Men , no doubt , might seek it long and late if our women ’ s minds no scheme devise .

sections.label: Section 482
sections.original: Φαίδρα , λέγει μὲν ἥδε χρησιμώτερα
sections.translation: Although she gives thee at thy present need the wiser counsel , Phaedra , yet do I praise thee . Still my praise may sound more harsh

sections.label: Section 485
sections.original: τῶν τῆσδε καὶ σοὶ μᾶλλον ἀλγίων κλύειν .
sections.translation: and jar more cruelly on thy ear than her advice .

sections.label: Section 486
sections.original: τοῦτ ’ ἔσθ ’ ὃ θνητῶν εὖ πόλεις οἰκουμένας
sections.translation: ’ Tis even this , too plausible a tongue , that overthrows good governments and homes of men . We should not speak to please the ear but point the path that leads to noble fame .

sections.label: Section 490
sections.original: τί σεμνομυθεῖς ; οὐ λόγων εὐσχημόνων
sections.translation: What means this solemn speech ? No need of rounded phrases ; [*] but at once must we sound the prince , telling him frankly how it is with thee . Had not thy life to such a crisis come , [*] or wert thou with self-control endowed ,

sections.label: Section 495
sections.original: οὐκ ἄν ποτ ’ εὐνῆς οὕνεχ ’ ἡδονῆς τε σῆς
sections.translation: ne ’ er would I to gratify thy passions have urged thee to this course ; but now ’ tis a struggle fierce to save thy life , and therefore less to blame .

sections.label: Section 498
sections.original: ὦ δεινὰ λέξασ ’, οὐχὶ συγκλῄσεις στόμα
sections.translation: Accursed proposal ! peace , woman ! never utter those shameful words again !

sections.label: Section 500
sections.original: αἴσχρ ’, ἀλλ ’ ἀμείνω τῶν καλῶν τάδ ’ ἐστί σοι .
sections.translation: Shameful , maybe , yet for thee better than honour ’ s code . Better this deed , if it shall save thy life , than that name thy pride will kill thee to retain .

sections.label: Section 503
sections.original: καὶ μή σε πρὸς θεῶν — εὖ λέγεις γάρ , αἰσχρὰ δέ —
sections.translation: I conjure thee , go no further ! for thy words are plausible but infamous ; for though as yet

sections.label: Section 505
sections.original: ψυχὴν ἔρωτι , τᾀσχρὰ δ ’ ἢν λέγῃς καλῶς ,
sections.translation: love has not [*] undermined my soul , yet , if in specious words thou dress thy foul suggestion , I shall be beguiled into the snare from which I am now escaping .

sections.label: Section 507
sections.original: εἴ τοι δοκεῖ σοι , χρῆν μὲν οὔ σ ’ ἁμαρτάνειν ·
sections.translation: If thou art of this mind , ’ twere well thou ne ’ er hadst sinned ; but as it is , hear me ; for that is the next best course ; I in my house have charms

sections.label: Section 510
sections.original: ἔρωτος , ἦλθε δ ’ ἄρτι μοι γνώμης ἔσω ,
sections.translation: to soothe thy love ,—’ twas but now I thought of them ;— these shall cure thee of thy sickness on no disgraceful terms , thy mind unhurt , if thou wilt be but brave .

sections.label: Section 513
sections.original: δεῖ δ ’ ἐξ ἐκείνου δή τι τοῦ ποθουμένου
sections.translation: But from him thou lovest we must get some token , a word or fragment of his robe ,

sections.label: Section 515
sections.original: λαβεῖν , συνάψαι τ ’ ἐκ δυοῖν μίαν χάριν .
sections.translation: and thereby unite in one love ’ s twofold stream . [*]

sections.label: Section 516
sections.original: πότερα δὲ χριστὸν ἢ ποτὸν τὸ φάρμακον ;
sections.translation: Is thy drug a salve or potion ?

sections.label: Section 517
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδ ’· ὀνάσθαι , μὴ μαθεῖν βούλου , τέκνον .
sections.translation: I cannot tell ; be content , my child , to profit by it and ask no questions .

sections.label: Section 518
sections.original: δέδοιχ ’ ὅπως μοι μὴ λίαν φανῇς σοφή .
sections.translation: I fear me thou wilt prove too wise for me .

sections.label: Section 519
sections.original: πάντ ’ ἂν φοβηθεῖσ ’ ἴσθι · δειμαίνεις δὲ τί ;
sections.translation: If thou fear this , confess thyself afraid of all ; but why thy terror ?

sections.label: Section 520
sections.original: μή μοί τι Θησέως τῶνδε μηνύσῃς τόκῳ .
sections.translation: Lest thou shouldst breathe a word of this to Theseus ’ son .

sections.label: Section 521
sections.original: ἔασον , ὦ παῖ · ταῦτ ’ ἐγὼ θήσω καλῶς .
sections.translation: Peace , my child ! I will do all things well ; only be thou , queen Cypris , ocean ’ s child , my partner in the work ! And for the rest of my purpose , it will be enough for me to tell it to Our friends within the house . [ Exit Nurse .

sections.label: Section 1.525
sections.original: Ἔρως Ἔρως , ὃ κατ ’ ὀμμάτων
sections.translation: O Love , Love , that from the eyes diffusest soft desire , bringing on the souls of those , whom thou dost camp against , sweet grace , O never in evil mood appear to me , nor out of time and tune approach !

sections.label: Section 1.530
sections.original: οὔτε γὰρ πυρὸς οὔτ ’ ἄστρων ὑπέρτερον βέλος ,
sections.translation: Nor fire nor meteor hurls a mightier bolt than Aphrodite ’ s shaft shot by the hands of Love , the child of Zeus .

sections.label: Section 1.535
sections.original: ἄλλως ἄλλως παρά τ ’ Ἀλφεῷ
sections.translation: Idly , idly by the streams of Alpheus and in the Pythian shrines of Phoebus , Hellas heaps the slaughtered steers ; while Love we worship not , Love , the king of men , who holds the key to Aphrodite ’ s

sections.label: Section 1.540
sections.original: φιλτάτων θαλάμων κλῃδοῦχον , οὐ σεβίζομεν ,
sections.translation: sweetest bower ,— worship not him who , when he comes , lays waste and marks his path to mortal hearts by wide-spread woe .

sections.label: Section 2.545
sections.original: τὰν μὲν Οἰχαλίᾳ
sections.translation: There was that maiden [*] in Oechalia , a girl unwed , that knew no wooer yet nor married joys ; her did the queen of Love [*] snatch from her home across the sea

sections.label: Section 2.550
sections.original: χαν σὺν αἵματι , σὺν καπνῷ
sections.translation: and gave unto Alcmena ’ s son , mid blood and smoke and murderous marriage-hymns , to be to him a frantic fiend of hell ; woe ! woe for his wooing !

sections.label: Section 2.555
sections.original: ὦ Θήβας ἱερὸν
sections.translation: Ah ! holy walls of Thebes , ah ! fount of Dirce , ye could testify what course the love-queen follows . For with the blazing levin-bolt

sections.label: Section 2.560
sections.original: χου νυμφευσαμένα πότμῳ
sections.translation: did she cut short the fatal marriage of Semele , mother of Zeus-bom Bacchus . All things she doth inspire , dread goddess , winging her flight hither and thither like a bee .

sections.label: Section 565
sections.original: σιγήσατ ’, ὦ γυναῖκες · ἐξειργάσμεθα .
sections.translation: Peace , ladies , peace ! I am undone .

sections.label: Section 566
sections.original: τί δ ’ ἔστι , Φαίδρα , δεινὸν ἐν δόμοισί σοι ;
sections.translation: What , Phaedra , is this dread event within thy house ?

sections.label: Section 567
sections.original: ἐπίσχετ ’, αὐδὴν τῶν ἔσωθεν ἐκμάθω .
sections.translation: Hush ! let me hear what those within are saying .

sections.label: Section 568
sections.original: σιγῶ · τὸ μέντοι φροίμιον κακὸν τόδε .
sections.translation: I am silent ; this is surely the prelude to mischief .

sections.label: Section 569
sections.original: ἰώ μοι , αἰαῖ ·
sections.translation: Great gods !

sections.label: Section 570
sections.original: ὦ δυστάλαινα τῶν ἐμῶν παθημάτων .
sections.translation: how awful are my sufferings !

sections.label: Section 571
sections.original: τίνα θροεῖς αὐδάν ; τίνα βοᾷς λόγον ;
sections.translation: What a cry was there ! what loud alarm ! say what sudden terror , lady , doth thy soul dismay .

sections.label: Section 575
sections.original: ἀπωλόμεσθα . ταῖσδ ’ ἐπιστᾶσαι πύλαις
sections.translation: I am undone . Stand here at the door and hear the noise arising in the house .

sections.label: Section 577
sections.original: σὺ παρὰ κλῇθρα , σοὶ μέλει πομπίμα
sections.translation: Thou art already by the bolted door ; ’ tis for thee to note the sounds that issue from within . And tell me , O tell me what mischief can be on foot .

sections.label: Section 581
sections.original: ὁ τῆς φιλίππου παῖς Ἀμαζόνος βοᾷ
sections.translation: ’ Tis the son of the horse-loving Amazon who calls , Hippolytus , uttering foul curses on my servant .

sections.label: Section 585
sections.original: ἀχὰν μὲν κλύω , σαφὲς δ ’ οὐκ ἔχω ·
sections.translation: I hear a noise , but cannot clearly tell [*] which way it comes . Ah ! ’ tis through the door the sound reached thee .

sections.label: Section 589
sections.original: καὶ μὴν σαφῶς γε τὴν κακῶν προμνήστριαν ,
sections.translation: Yes , yes , he is calling her plainly enough a go-between in vice ,

sections.label: Section 590
sections.original: τὴν δεσπότου προδοῦσαν ἐξαυδᾷ λέχος .
sections.translation: traitress to her master ’ s honour .

sections.label: Section 591
sections.original: ὤμοι ἐγὼ κακῶν · προδέδοσαι , φίλα .
sections.translation: Woe , woe is me ! thou art betrayed , dear mistress ! What counsel shall I give thee ? thy secret is out ; thou art utterly undone .

sections.label: Section 594
sections.original: αἰαῖ , ἒ ἔ .
sections.translation: Ah me ! ah me !

sections.label: Section 595
sections.original: πρόδοτος ἐκ φίλων .
sections.translation: Betrayed by friends !

sections.label: Section 596
sections.original: ἀπώλεσέν μ ’ εἰποῦσα συμφορὰς ἐμάς ,
sections.translation: She hath ruined me by speaking of my misfortune ; ’ twas kindly meant , but an ill way to cure my malady .

sections.label: Section 598
sections.original: πῶς οὖν ; τί δράσεις , ὦ παθοῦσ ’ ἀμήχανα ;
sections.translation: O what wilt thou do now in thy cruel dilemma ?
